headline: Russia holds scaled-back Victory Day parade as Putin denounces NATO
summary: President Putin used his annual Victory Day speech to justify the 'special military operation' in Ukraine. The parade was the most scaled-back in years due to security threats, occurring during a temporary 3-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.

headline: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure' as tensions escalate
summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated the US attacks each time there is a diplomatic solution on the table. The US fired on and disabled two more Iranian tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, exchanging fire with Iranian forces.

headline: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius approaches Tenerife
summary: The cruise ship carrying passengers affected by a hantavirus outbreak is expected to dock in Tenerife, Canary Islands. WHO officials are drawing up guidance for the nearly 150 passengers. Evacuation must be completed within 24 hours due to incoming bad weather.

headline: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's new Prime Minister
summary: Péter Magyar was sworn in as Hungary's new PM, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán. His Tisza party won a landslide victory nearly a month ago, marking a significant political shift in Hungary.

headline: International cyber attack disrupts universities and schools via Canvas
summary: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for breaching Canvas, an academic software platform used by thousands of schools and universities globally. The attack disrupted final exams and deprived students of essential materials.

headline: Pope Leo XIV marks first anniversary with prayer for peace
summary: Pope Leo XIV marked his first anniversary as head of the Catholic Church with a prayer for peace at the Pompeii shrine. He asked God to inspire world leaders to calm global tensions and reduce hatred, framing war as a failure of human solidarity.

headline: Mount Dukono volcano erupts in Indonesia killing three hikers
summary: An explosive eruption of Mount Dukono on Halmahera Island killed three hikers, with others missing from a group of 20 who defied official warnings. The volcano is one of Indonesia's most active, with increased activity noted since late March.

headline: Mystery naval drone with explosives found in Greece's Ionian Sea
summary: Greek authorities are examining a mystery naval drone found in the Ionian Sea. Greek media report the drone had explosives and could be either Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine war.

headline: US government releases UFO documents with transcripts and video
summary: The Pentagon published transcripts, video clips and audio recordings about unidentified flying objects. The US was unable to make a definitive determination on alleged UFO sightings filmed around the world.

headline: Russia-Ukraine 3-day ceasefire takes effect amid low-key Victory Day
summary: A temporary three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine took effect, coinciding with Russia's Victory Day commemorations. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the US is awaiting Iran's response over proposals for a broader ceasefire deal.
